12.reason: The base case check is placed after the recursive loop, causing it to never be reached because the loop will always execute first and recurse infinitely. It should be placed before the loop to properly terminate recursion.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The base case check is placed after the recursive loop, causing it to never be reached because the loop will always execute first and recurse infinitely. It should be placed before the loop to properly terminate recursion.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The condition check is placed after the loop, causing the base case to be executed after all recursive calls return, but the loop will also run when t==n+1, leading to incorrect behavior because the loop accesses x[t] and y[i] with t out of valid range (t=n+1, but x and y are indexed from 1 to n). The base case should be checked before the loop to properly terminate recursion.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The recursion base case should be placed at the beginning of the function to properly terminate; also missing a 'return' statement after printing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  This check is placed after the loop, causing infinite recursion as the function never returns when t == n+1.

4.reason: Declaration of loop variable inside for is not allowed in C89, causing compilation error.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Declaration of loop variable inside for is not allowed in C89, causing compilation error.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Compilation error: variable declaration inside for loop is not allowed in C89; use separate declaration int i;]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  [C89/C90 standard does not allow variable declaration inside for-loop initialization, causing a compilation error in strict/older compilers]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  The base case condition should be checked before this loop to prevent infinite recursion.
